title: Frankenstein; or, the modern prometheus
author: Shelley, Mary Wollstonecraft
summary: An ambitious Arctic explorer, Robert Walton writes to his sister Margaret from St. Petersburgh and Archangel, detailing preparations for a perilous voyage toward the North Pole. He describes his enthusiasm for discovery, years of training with whale-fishers and study, and his longing for an intimate friend to share intellectual sympathy. Walton recruits a gentle, courageous master and an English lieutenant noted for noble self-sacrifice. He reflects on solitude, romantic impulses stirred by poets, and the hazards of ocean travel. Expectant and anxious, he sets sail hoping glory and scientific revelation await, while admitting that success or death may separate him.

characters.name: Robert Walton
characters.personality: ambitious, romantic, intellectually curious, lonely and determined
characters.lifespan: alive in the letters; embarking on an Arctic voyage; fate described as uncertain

characters.name: Margaret Saville (Mrs. Saville)
characters.personality: sisterly, gentle, the primary addressee and emotional anchor
characters.lifespan: alive in England; recipient of Walton's letters; fate unspecified in provided text

characters.name: Uncle Thomas
characters.personality: collector of voyages; provided Walton with reading material in childhood

characters.name: Cousin (unnamed)
characters.personality: benefactor by bequest (source of Walton's inheritance)

characters.name: Walton's Father
characters.personality: author of a dying injunction mentioned by Walton

characters.name: Master (ship's master)
characters.personality: gentle, mild in discipline, honest and courageous
characters.lifespan: serves on Walton's vessel; fate unspecified

characters.name: Lieutenant (unnamed)
characters.personality: dauntless, noble, reserved, noted for self-sacrifice and kindliness
characters.lifespan: engaged to serve under Walton; present in letters; fate unspecified

characters.name: Captain (unnamed, of whale-vessel)
characters.personality: experienced seaman who once offered Walton promotion
characters.lifespan: appears in Walton's past account; fate unspecified

characters.name: Young Russian lady (unnamed)
characters.personality: emotional; preferred another suitor and wept before a marriage ceremony
characters.lifespan: married according to her inclinations later; otherwise unspecified

characters.name: Her lover / rival (unnamed)
characters.personality: poor but beloved by the young Russian lady; recipient of the lieutenant's generosity
characters.lifespan: received farm and means from lieutenant; fate unspecified

characters.name: Old man (father of the Russian lady)
characters.personality: rigid, inexorable in honour and refusal to consent
characters.lifespan: rejected the match initially; fate unspecified

characters.name: A lady who told the lieutenant's story (beneficiary)
characters.personality: grateful; credited the lieutenant with restoring her happiness
characters.lifespan: appears as narrator of the lieutenant's backstory; fate unspecified

locations.name: Cape of Good Hope (referred to as southern cape of Africa)
Cape Horn (referred to as southern cape of America)

unusual_words.context: Letter 1: Walton addresses his sister about her 'evil forebodings' regarding his expedition.

Letter 1: Walton speaks of 'those icy climes' toward which he is advancing.
Letter 1: the northern breeze gives him a 'foretaste' of the icy regions.
